observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the Implementing Offices or focal persons to retrieve and turn-over available records to the City Treasurer and City Accountant to facilitate formulation of appropriate strategies to recover the amount of fund transfers to NGOs/POs. Also, we recommend that Management, through the City Accountant and City Treasurer, send notices to the NGOs/POs to enforce submission of liquidation reports and adopt all possible remedies to collect these receivables. Accordingly, the City Government would have additional funds to finance more developmental projects.
observations.managementAction: The City coordinated with the various NGOs/POs and required them to submit financial/liquidation reports on the funds they received.
observations.reason: An interview with the CAO revealed that most of the NGOs/POs were no longer existing and there are no records available thus, they cannot provide liquidation report. Moreover, no collection notices or demand letters were sent to the existing NGOs/POs. This is reiterated in Observation No. 5 page 71 of this report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that Management:

Require the BAC to review all contracts pertaining to infrastructure projects and Consulting Services to ascertain the target completion date, monitor contract time expiration, and implement calibrated actions provided in GPPB Circular No. 03-2019 before considering termination, either in part or in full, of any incomplete work not yet accepted under the contract;

Impose administrative sanctions, if warranted, in accordance with Section 69 of the IRR of RA No. 9184, to protect the interest of the government and to prevent similar occurrence of extended delays in project implementation. Also, apply the appropriate Liquidated Damages for the unperformed portion of the contract for each day of delay.

Direct the City Engineer to conduct necessary field surveys and investigations in detailed engineering and carefully study/evaluate the site conditions in the preparation of accurate design plans, technical specifications, quantity and cost estimates, and program of work for infrastructure projects before implementation to minimize any work suspension that may cause delays in project completion.

Release RWO to lift the Suspension Work Order issued and allowing the contractor to continue the remaining unfinished work to fully complete the project, thereby preventing any delay in the benefits that could be derived therefrom.
observations.managementAction:
The City Planning and Development Office with the aid of City Engineering Office (CEO) monitored the ongoing projects to further undertake the effective and efficient implementation of the projects within their target completion period.





Management actively imposed sanctions such as Liquidated damages for delays.





The CEO conducted necessary fields surveys and investigations in detailed engineering and carefully studied/ evaluated the site conditions in preparation of accurate design, plans, technical specifications, quantity and cost estimates, and program of works for infrastructure projects before implementation.
observations.reason: No action of management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended management to prevent the incurrence of penalties arising from delayed payments of water consumption and to require the responsible officials/employees who have access to and control over the financial affairs of the LGU to pay/refund the penalties resulting from their inability to pay the bills on or before the due date.
observations.managementAction: Management coordinated with MVWD to ensure that penalties were not included in the payment of water consumption.
observations.reason: Verification of vouchers showed that penalties for delays were included in the payment of water consumption of Vigan City Slaughterhouse and Collado 2.

Also, ensure strict adherence to Sections 1 and 2 of AO No. 141, s 1994 to avoid any administrative sanctions as provided in Section 3 of AO No. 141. Furthermore, refrain from using government funds for purposes other than public purposes as provided in Section 4, item 2 of PD No. 1445 to support the validity and propriety of the transaction.

observations.reason: Payments of insurance for all insurable properties of the City for 2024 were still to a private company

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management ensure implementation of the development projects which are harmonized with the Regional Development Plan and the Philippine Development Plan and monitor the proper implementation of the PPAs funded under the 20% DF for development projects to maximize the utilization of the fund as intended and ultimately achieve desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes in the City Government as provided under DBM-DOF-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 1 dated November 4, 2020.
observations.managementAction: The Management reviewed and prioritized projects and established quarterly monitoring and evaluation meetings to assess project progress and discussed challenges.
observations.reason: Utilization rate of 20% DF was only 42% and 25.53% of the total amount for current and continuing appropriation, respectively. This is reiterated in Observation No. 12, page 94 of this report.

observations.recommendation:
In order to fully utilize the long-outstanding NGAs’ funds, the Audit Team recommended that the City Mayor instruct the:
City Health Officer to immediately plan for projects that should be funded by the Capitation, TB-DOTS, Maternity Care Package, and Animal Bite Package Facility Fund to avoid further idling of these funds, thus benefits will be enjoyed by the constituents of the city. Likewise, release the professional fees to the intended beneficiaries;
City Accountant to facilitate the return of balances of funds to the different source agencies concerned. Make a list or summary of the items for adjustment or reversion for ready reference;
City Accountant to remind the recipient agencies/offices to facilitate the utilization/liquidation of the awards and prizes to enjoy the benefits of their good work otherwise, adjust the same as income of the City Government.
City Treasurer to remit to the PNP their share of the Police Inspection Fee.
The Audit Team recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City Accountant to include the subject fund in the summary or list of funds identified for adjustment or reversion, to benefit from the fund and prevent it from remaining idle.
The Audit Team recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City Accountant to prepare the necessary documents and voucher and the City Treasurer to effect the actual transfer of funds from GF to SEF or TF and from TF to GF to correct errors made and to eliminate the Due to Other Funds account. Henceforth, see to it that errors are immediately corrected and actual transfer of funds as the case maybe to avoid unadjusted balance that will appear under the Due to Other Funds account at year end for a fair presentation of the financial statements.
The Audit Team recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City Accountant to coordinate with the GSO, City Engineer, BAC Secretariat and City Treasurer to analyze the composition of the Guaranty/Security Deposits Payable account and determine possibility of refund to contractors/suppliers/dealers and the individuals who paid the cash bonds, excavation/conservation bonds and bidder’s bonds. Effect any adjustment in the books if processing of refunds is not possible.
The Audit Team recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City Accountant to include in the list or summary of items for adjustment or reversion the collections, affiliation fees, proceeds from dental services, prizes, financial assistance and other fees from the Other Payables and Trust Liabilities accounts to Miscellaneous Operating and Service Income and Income from Grants and Donations.
The Audit Team therefore recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City Accountant to coordinate with the concerned departments to review all schedules of the various liability accounts. These accounts should be consolidated for possible reversion and subsequently forwarded to the City Mayor. The City Mayor may then request authority from the Sangguniang Panlungsod (SP) for the reversion of such accounts to the unappropriated surplus, thus allowing the City Government to utilize the funds for other developmental projects.

The Audit Team recommended that the City Mayor:
direct the City Engineer and City Planning and Development Coordinator (CPDC) to verify the existence of valid grounds for termination and ensure the preparation of a Verified Report, which will serve as the basis for the City Mayor’s decision to terminate the contract, provided that a written notice of such termination has been properly conveyed to the contractor or consultant; and
instruct the BAC to promulgate a resolution recommending to the City Mayor the adoption of Negotiated Procurement for the continuation of projects of which contracts have been terminated, in order to immediately act on the resumption and completion of these projects and mitigate delays.

The Audit Team recommended that the City Mayor:
instruct the CGSO to coordinate with PCSO and PAGCOR to obtain documentation on the fair value of the donated vehicles at the time of donation, and to furnish the CAO with such documents for proper recording;
direct the Inventory Committee, in cases where the fair value cannot be obtained from the donors, to determine the appraised value of the donated properties as of December 31, 2024, and submit the appraisal report to the City Accountant; and
instruct the City Accountant to recognize the donated assets in the books of accounts at their fair value or appraised value, less accumulated depreciation, if applicable, using the following pro forma journal entries:
To record the donated motor vehicles (as prior period error correction) at fair value at the date of donation:
Dr.		Motor Vehicles			xx
Cr.		Government Equity			xx
To record the missed depreciation for CY 2023:
Dr.		Government Equity			xx
Cr.		Accumulated Depreciation			xx

The Audit Team recommended that Management instruct the City Accountant to:
initiate exhaustive collection effort such as issuance of demand letters and actual visitation of the different NGOs/POs specially the existing ones and file the request for write off for those which can no longer be traced in accordance with COA Circular No. 2023-008;
maintain complete records for all present and subsequent receivable accounts for enhanced monitoring and purposive actions requiring timely settlements to prevent the occurrence of past due receivables; and
conduct regular and periodic verification, analysis and validation of existence of NGOs/POs with outstanding loans and undertake remedial and preventive actions to manage unsettled accounts.

The Audit Team recommended that the City Mayor:
direct the CGSO to immediately facilitate the filing of the Request for Relief from Accountability, and ensure that appropriate justification is provided for the delayed submission for proper evaluation and appropriate action; and
henceforth require all relevant personnel to ensure the timely reporting and proper disposition of lost, damaged, or unserviceable assets, and to comply fully with asset management procedures.

The Audit Team recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Engineer to properly monitor the posting of signboards with the required information by the contractor at every project site of the City’s infrastructure projects to ensure that the public is provided with relevant information about the project's existence and key details, thereby promoting transparency and accountability on the implementation of awarded contracts with the end in view that these contracts are performed according to specifications enunciated therein.

The Audit Team recommended that the City Mayor instruct the Local Finance Committee to reassess the idle funds held in time deposit accounts and consider closing the same to make the funds available for reappropriation and immediate use by the City Government. Alternatively, the Audit Team suggested the passing of a new SP resolution that specifies, among other provisions and the maturity dates of the time deposit accounts. This resolution should ensure that the funds remain invested long enough to generate the desired interest income while guaranteeing that both the principal and interest are promptly utilized upon maturity.

The Audit Team recommended that the City Mayor instruct the CHRMO and the City Accountant to verify and reconcile with GSIS the component of the unremitted balances and to remit the same immediately. Henceforth, report immediately to GSIS any changes or updates on the City’s personnel file to avoid discrepancies between the payroll deductions and the EBCS billing file.
Further, the Audit Team recommended that the City Mayor consider discussing the issue during the Mayors League meeting and make a resolution, furnish the same to the National Mayors League for a possible representation to the GSIS, hence unaccepted remittance due to differences with the EBCS billing file would be addressed.

The Audit Team recommended that the City Mayor secure authorization from the SP before entering into contracts for specific projects funded by lump sum appropriations under the annual budget’s generic PPAs of which the request for approval includes the project description, estimated cost, and the corresponding generic PPA to ensure that each project is properly charged to a lump sum appropriation which requires SP approval for transparency and accountability.

The Audit Team recommended that the City Mayor direct the Head of the BAC Secretariat to require relevant end-users or implementing units to prepare the PPMPs, subject to evaluation by the City Budget Officer, during the APP formulation in order to eliminate the establishment of generic PPAs, thus, ensuring timely procurement, efficient resource management, enhanced transparency, and compliance with legal requirements, ultimately benefiting the public.

The Audit Team recommended that the City Mayor instruct the implementing offices to carefully prepare the ADP by identifying the specific PPAs and preparing the corresponding POWs, technical specifications and other necessary documents prior to the inclusion of PPAs in the plan.
The Audit Team also recommended that the City Mayor refrain from including generic PPAs and instruct the CPDC to coordinate with other implementing offices to prepare a monitoring report for all unimplemented generic projects and determine and evaluate if PPAs are specific and still viable and immediately implement the same, otherwise reprogram the fund for other priority and specific projects thus, the benefits will be timely delivered to the constituents.

The Audit Team recommended that the City Mayor require the GAD Focal Person to carefully and properly prepare the GPB and coordinate with other concerned agencies to ensure that there is no duplication of PAPs so that all of the gender issues identified are addressed and resolved.
Furthermore, the Audit Team recommended that the City Mayor establish a GAD Monitoring and Evaluation Team through the issuance of an EO or AO, in compliance with Section 5 of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No 2013-01, as amended by JMC No. 2016-01, to ensure that GAD PAPs are given focus and prioritize accordingly, thus, the constituents could timely enjoy the benefits derived from them.

The Audit Team recommended that the City Mayor direct the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Officer to ensure that the PPAs funded by the LDRRMF will be fully implemented in the year they are appropriated thus, the necessary skills, knowledge and materials/equipment to strengthen responsiveness of the City and its constituents during and after disasters are timely delivered.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to immediately enforce the settlement of the audit disallowances and charges as prescribed under the aforementioned Circular, to ensure that funds are recovered and used efficiently for the benefit of the constituents.

We recommended that the LCE require the City Accountant to prepare and certify the correctness of the Monthly Report on Utilization of LDRRMF together with Detailed Report of Actual Charges in adherence to Section 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 and to support the accuracy of the Trust Liability-DRRMF account in the financial statements. Also, direct the CDRRMO and City Accountant to reconcile their reports to ensure accurate information on the unutilized balance of LDRRMF. The reconciled balance serves as the basis for management in formulating appropriate plan to support DRRM PPAs.
observations.managementAction: The City Accountant prepared and certified the correctness of the monthly report on utilization of LDRRMF together with detailed Report of Actual Charges.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management through the City Accountant to use the prescribed account titles as set forth in COA Circular No. 2020-004 to provide accurate information for the fair presentation of the related accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The City Accountant complied with the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE invite the attention of the City Social Development Officer to adopt an updated policies and guidelines of AICS program to ensure that the financial assistance granted is valid and regular.

observations.reason: Financial assistance under AICS program were granted to beneficiaries without adhering to the established rates of assistance provided for by EO 02-A Series of 2016, thus defeating the objectives of the EO to aid individuals in times of distress and need by receiving a reasonable amount of assistance.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the LCE that the City Social Development Officer strict adhere to the rates of financial assistance for AICS beneficiaries as provided for by the existing EO. Otherwise, the LCE should formally amend/modify it, or to promulgate a new EO, in accordance with the procedural requirements of law. For the determination of the rates of assistance, to provide clear guidelines that are in-line with the memorandum issued by the DSWD for AICS Program, thus due assistance and social justice may be served to qualified beneficiaries.
observations.managementAction: The City Social Development Officer strictly complied with the prescribed rates of financial assistance under the new EO No. 32.
observations.reason: The rates under the new EO were followed strictly as per verification of vouchers.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that henceforth, Management strictly identify the priority projects if it is beyond their capability to execute such project before granting the award to the NGOs/POs for implementation, which shall be clearly defined in the MOA and authorize the grant of financial assistance to NGOs/POs based on existing law, rules and regulations to support the validity and propriety of the transactions.

observations.reason: Financial and other types of assistance granted to farmers were made by either cash advance or direct to the supplier and not to the NGOs/POs.

observations.recommendation: We recommended management to instruct the LDRRMO to utilize the LDRRMF in line with the projects and activities provided in Section 5 of NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC No. 2013-1, dated March 25, 2013, to support disaster risk management activities aimed at mitigating, preparing for, responding to, and recovering from the impact of the disasters.
observations.managementAction: The Management LDRRM complied with Section 5 of NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC No. 2013-1.
observations.reason: No MAAB was charged during the year under audit. Verification of the Report on the Utilization of LDRRMF showed that expenses were in line with Section 5 of the said Circular.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management require the BAC to strictly evaluate the legal and technical requirements submitted by the prospective bidders, as provided in Section 23.4.1.3 of the Updated 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184. Additionally, if after conducting the eligibility check, all the bidders failed to meet the eligibility requirements, the BAC shall declare a failure of bidding. Lastly, the BAC’s recommendation to the Head of the Procuring Entity (HoPE) must be supported with complete data and accurate information, ensuring the validity and propriety of the contract to be definite and undisputed.

observations.reason: Checklist of/and legal and technical requirements were attached to the voucher.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE direct the BAC to observe due diligence in evaluating the documents submitted by bidders to avoid suspicion of favoring specific contractors to the detriment of the public interest and in order to ensure transparency and integrity of the procurement process.
observations.managementAction: The BAC strictly adhered to the recommendation that the BAC thoroughly evaluate the legal and technical requirements as provided under RA No. 9184.

Not Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management direct the BAC Secretariat to post all Procurement Opportunities on the PhilGEPS website, the website of the Procuring Entity concerned, if available, and at any conspicuous places reserved for this purpose in the premises of the Procuring Entity for a period of at least three calendar days. Additionally, ensure that NOA and Contract/PO are posted within ten days of their issuance, as provided in IV-E and L of Annex “H” of the 2016 Revised IRR of R.A No. 9184 to promote transparency and efficiency in the procurement process, providing equal opportunity to all eligible and qualified suppliers.
observations.managementAction: The BAC Secretariat adhered to the recommendation to post invitation to bid and notice of award including contracts/PO in PhilGEPS Website, Vigan City Website and conspicuous places of the agency, particularly at the bulletin board of Vigan City Hall within the prescribed calendar days.
observations.reason: Printed screenshots of postings of Invitations to Bid in PhilGEPS and Vigan City website were attached to the vouchers as well as certification signed by the BAC Secretary that Invitation to Bid, contract, NOA and NTP were posted at any conspicuous place of the City.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE require the BAC to recommend, through a BAC Resolution, the adoption of suitable alternative modes of procurement for his consideration as the HoPE These methods should be chosen based on the conditions and limitations outlined in Annex “H” to enhance transparency, competitiveness, economy, and efficiency in the procurement process, thereby ensuring the City Government secures the most advantageous prices.
observations.managementAction: The BAC ensured that the rules, regulations, and terms and conditions for the use of Alternative Methods of Procurement for Goods, Infrastructure Projects, and Consulting Services are implemented to promote competitiveness.
observations.reason: Post audit of vouchers showed that suitable alternative modes of procurement were adopted based on rules and regulations and limitations on the modes. of procurement.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management allocate not less than five percent of the estimated revenue from regular sources for LDRRMF in order to fully achieve the objective of mainstreaming disaster risk reduction and lessening the socioeconomic and environmental impacts of disasters at all levels in the local community. Also, we recommend Management to invite the attention of the City Accountant and the City Public Safety Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer to reconcile their records to reflect the correct amount of LDRRMF in the Financial Statements.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the General Services Office to monitor the records for the timely registration to avoid incurring penalties and surcharges arising from delayed registration. observations.reason: There were no penalties paid for registration of vehicles for the year 2024.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management and other concerned officials ensure that programmed development projects funded under the 20% DF comply with the JMC and are optimally utilized to help achieve the desired socio-economic targets and outcomes of the LGU. observations.managementAction: The Management reprogrammed the allotted amount to other development programs of the City in compliance with Sec 3.2 of DBM-DOF-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 1 dated November 4, 2020.
observations.reason: Review of the report of utilization of 20% DF for 2024 showed that in compliance with the AOM, the Management did not implement the program of granting financial assistance to constituents.

drop from the PPE accounts all the items below ₱15,000.00 by preparing the necessary adjusting entries;
make the necessary adjusting entries to capitalize the police equipment and gadgets totaling ₱2,045,680.00, and the prior year’s expense by the same amount; and
record the unissued police equipment and gadgets as Military Police and Traffic Supplies Inventory amounting to ₱141,040.00.

2.6.	We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City Accountant to:
maintain complete records for all present and subsequent receivable accounts for enhanced monitoring and purposive actions requiring timely settlements to prevent the occurrence of past due receivables;
conduct regular and periodic verification, analysis, and validation of the existence of receivables and undertake remedial and preventive actions in the management of unsettled accounts; and
determine the final status of the long outstanding receivable accounts, make a compilation of the relevant records and proof of exhaustive collection efforts such as demand letters and actual visitation/interview of the different cooperatives/individuals, and initiate the request for write-off per COA Circular No. 2016-005.

We recommended that the City Mayor request for the discontinuance and reversion of the continuing appropriations from CY 2019 and prior thereto to the unappropriated surplus in pursuance of Section 4 (a) of the appropriation ordinance for the annual budget, and continue to abide by the same for the next years.

4.5.	We recommended that the City Mayor state the reasons or justifications for the discontinuance of the PPAs no longer needed along with the recommendation for the reversion of the related funds to the SP. Only after approval thereof by the SP through a resolution that the funds will be considered as savings and later on become a funding source of a subsequent supplemental budget.

5.5.	We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City Accountant to prepare a Journal Entry Voucher for the capitalization of the amount disbursed from the GF in the SEF to Other Structures to effect the correct project cost of the handwashing facilities for the 21 public schools.

6.9.	We recommended that the City Mayor consider the return of the unused balance of the program by NSCC and propose addendums/amendments to the MOA which include the following:
To indicate clearly the terms and conditions, such as the interest rate and its coverage to ensure that loan applicants are properly informed of the true interest rate of the loan to be availed of;
To provide a provision as to the duration for the implementation of the program which may necessarily coincide with that of the State of Calamity as declared by the President so that full collection may be achieved the soonest possible; and
To clearly indicate if the loans partially collected by NSCC may be reloaned to other loan applicants endorsed by the agency or to be returned to it.
6.10.	We further recommended that the City Mayor seek approval from the Sangguniang Panlungsod for the creation of a special account once the balance of the program is returned to the agency. The city government would just have to disburse to NSCC the amount corresponding to the approved loan so that what may be left for NSCC to do is the release of the amount after service fees and insurance has been deducted, and the collection and return of the loan including the interest earned to the agency after they fall due.

7.9.	We recommended that the City Mayor:
plan its procurement with the end goal that resources are managed, expended and utilized in accordance with law and regulations and safeguarded against loss or wastage through illegal or improper disposition, with a view to ensuring efficiency, economy and effectiveness in the operations of government;
require the City Internal Auditor to facilitate the establishment of controls or measures necessary to ensure that all goods procured for distribution/issuance to the intended recipient or end-user are actually distributed/issued; and
require the GSO to issue a new Inventory Custodian Slip or Property Acknowledgment Receipt covering the actual quantity of goods issued to the PNP-SWAT.

8.4.	We recommended that the City Mayor require the CSWD Officer and City Accountant to coordinate with each other for the immediate release of ESA to the identified beneficiaries who have completed the required documents. Likewise, require the CSWD Officer for possible and immediate completion of the required documents, consider the basis of the Accounting Office in their review of the documentation, and evaluate/decide as to their eligibility for the program, in order to facilitate the release of the ESA to the other identified beneficiaries.

9.6.	We recommended that the City Mayor, require the coordinated efforts of the Inventory Committee, Accounting, General Services and other concerned offices, to comply with the guidelines and procedures enunciated in COA Circular No. 2020-006 in order to address the issue of having recurring and enormous discrepancies in its PPE accounts to come up with reliable PPE balances that are verifiable as to existence, condition and accountability.

10.11.	We recommended that the City Mayor require the City Planning and Development Officer (CPDO) to:
indicate the specific proposed projects for funding out of the tobacco excise tax fund for immediate implementation and enable the farmers to reap the benefits due them; and
ensure that the projects are feasible and sustainable and conduct regular monitoring to validate progress of the projects so that the objectives of the fund are achieved.

11.12.	We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City Development Council to:
include in the ADP and AIP only those projects which could surely be implemented during the year to maximize the utilization of the 20% DF;
revisit the unimplemented PPAs and review its viability and necessity. In this manner, optimal utilization of the fund may also be achieved; and
identify in sufficient detail the development projects to be funded by the 20% DF such that the only thing left for the City Mayor to do is just to execute the related contract. By this means, optimal utilization of the fund could also be attained, not to mention the benefits that could be derived from the immediate implementation of the projects.

12.6.	We recommended that the City Mayor require the GSO to:
ensure that all properties (e.g., firearms, gears and equipment) held by PNP-SWAT including PNP-EOD are properly managed by conducting timely and surprise inspection;
include also in the deed the list of PNP-SWAT members who are the end-users of the firearms to establish accountability and for them to be held solidarily liable with the Chief of Police in the event of misuse, destruction or loss thereof. Likewise, issue the corresponding PAR to the Chief of Police who will be primarily accountable and responsible for the firearms, equipment and other items subject of the deed;
provide copies of relevant documents to PNP-SWAT, such as the PAR, ICS, and the Deed of Usufruct, among others, to amplify control over the properties and items owned by the agency, and to serve as a reminder of the existence of an agreement between the City Government and the Chief of Police;
issue PAR covering the properties and other items totaling ₱4,876,495.00, summarized as follows:
d.1.   Firearms – ₱2,348,235.00
d.2.   Various equipment and gadgets erroneously recorded as
OMOE – ₱2,045,680.00
d.3.   Other procurements of equipment and gadgets – ₱482,580.00; and
be apprised of the correct forms to be attached in the issuance of supplies, properties and equipment to end-users.
12.7.	With regard to the procurements of goods which are similar in nature, we recommended that the City Mayor direct the BAC to group such items into a single procurement activity. By this means, procurements may be effected in a manner that is most advantageous to the agency and also avoid unwarranted resort to alternative modes of procurement.

14.5.	We recommended that the City Mayor direct the BAC to consolidate the requisitions for one or more items needed at or about the same time by the requisitioner in order to prevent the unnecessary duplication of procurement process and inefficient use of resources.

15.6.	We recommended that the City Mayor require the GSO to comply with the prompt submission of copies of the AIRs to the audit team in order for the latter to conduct timely inspection and verification of deliveries for agency’s benefit.

16.7.	We recommended that the City Mayor make representation with “The New Benjo’s Drugstore” for the implementation of RA No. 9994 and RA No. 10754. Likewise, instruct/direct the concerned officers with regard to the checking of the prescriptions to regularly remind the eligible clients to always bring with them their SC or PWD cards and purchase slip booklet when proceeding to the designated drugstore to get medicines in order to avail of the 20% discount. Moreover, ensure that the age of the client is indicated in the prescription so as to identify immediately who is entitled for the discount.

We recommended that Management instruct the Accounting Office to:
Maintain complete records for all present and subsequent receivable accounts for enhanced monitoring and purposive actions requiring timely settlements to prevent the occurrence of past due receivables;
Conduct regular and periodic verification, analysis and validation of existence of receivables and undertake remedial and preventive actions in the management of  unsettled accounts; and
Determine the final status of the long outstanding receivable accounts, make a compilation of the relevant records and proof of exhaustive collection efforts and initiate the request for write-off in accordance with COA Circular 2016-005.

We therefore recommended that, for the fair presentation of the account balances in the financial statements, the City Accountant restore the cash equivalent of all unreleased checks by debiting Cash in Bank – LCCA and crediting the appropriate payable/liability accounts supported with a working paper at the end of the month or quarter and by preparing a JEV at the end of the year based on the submitted schedule from the City Treasurer.
We also recommended that the City Treasurer submit the unclaimed checks together with the covering vouchers and supporting documents to the Accounting Office if it had been six months after the issuance of the checks to serve as basis for recording stale checks and for the issuance of replacement/s, as needed.

We therefore recommended that Management require the City Treasurer to reduce/decrease the inventory level of cash tickets in her custody in such manner that it will only be approximately twice the average monthly issuance of cash tickets and this will be the amount to be applied for the maximum accountability of custody of cash tickets in her fidelity bond.
We recommended further that Management cancel the fidelity bond coverage for cash tickets of those accountable officers who were not actually handling the same and provide sufficient coverage for the cash collections of the sixteen (16) collectors to protect the interest of the City Government and its constituents.

We, therefore, recommended that the City Government strictly follow the provisions of RA 656 in acquiring insurance coverage for all its insurable properties to avoid suspensions and/or disallowances in audit and other penalties as prescribed under the law.
Having records for the comparison of the insurance premiums quoted by a private insurer with that of GSIS, it is also recommended that the city officials or employees concerned furnish the latter with the premium rates assessed by the former to serve as reference so that they may consider adjusting their quotation to a competitive level or lower than private companies.

We therefore recommended that the requisitioning office or departments prepare duly accomplished Requisition Issue Slip and submit the same to the General Services Office for the issuance of their consumable supplies. It is further recommended that the GSO limit the use of Inventory Custodian Slips to those tangible assets with serviceable life of more than one year but small enough to be considered as PPE.

We recommended that a comprehensive Annual Procurement Plan be prepared consistent with the Annual Budget and once the APP is final and approved, it shall be submitted to the GPPB on or before the end of January of the budget year and it shall also be posted in the GPPB website. In this way, transparency as a governing principle in public procurement and compliance with the rule of law are recognized and adopted. Moreover, we recommended that the Procuring Entity and the BAC strictly adhere to all provisions of law, rules and regulations pertaining to procurement since the interest of the city government, being a political subdivision of the government, should at all times be protected.

We recommended that the City Government procure its common-use supplies and equipment from the PS-DBM to facilitate the procurement process and do away with the rigors of public bidding with private entities and also to avail of low-priced goods and the best value for money.

We recommended that the Accounting Office see to it that all claims are duly supported with complete documentary requirements to adhere with the provision set forth under Section 4 (6) of PD No. 1445, otherwise known as the State Audit Code of the Philippines, to avoid possible suspensions and disallowances in audit.

We recommended that the Sangguniang Panlungsod pass a resolution authorizing the City Mayor and City Treasurer to open a separate bank account for the Share from Tobacco Excise Tax. Further, the City Government should maintain separate books to facilitate monitoring and evaluation of the utilization of the fund.

We recommended that the City Government be guided by COA-DBM-DILG-GCG-DND Joint Circular No. 2015-01 dated January 8, 2015 as regards the release, use, reporting and submission for audit of confidential funds and strictly follow the procedural guidelines provided therein to prevent mishandling or misapplication.

We recommended that the Local School Board (LSB) be guided by the provisions of RA No. 7160 and DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017 in the preparation of the SEF Annual Budget and identify the priority expenditures pursuant thereto. We also recommended that the City Government stop the charging of the aforementioned expenditures to the Special Education Fund and instead utilize the funds for the priority needs of the schools identified in the above provisions of the law.
In addition, it is recommended that the SEF be strictly utilized by the LSB for the operation and maintenance of public schools only which shall mean government financed and managed schools and should not include private educational institutions as well as state universities which have their own budget for said expenditures.

We recommended that the LDRRM Officer submit monthly Reports on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF on time and observe the timely submission of the annual LDRRMFIP. We likewise recommend the provision of opportunities for capacity building trainings/seminars to personnel of the CDRRMO for improved reporting.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the City Social Welfare and Development Office to strictly abide by the terms and conditions of   the Memorandum of Agreement in the implementation of its livelihood program.     If their intention was to provide materials rather than cash to implement the said program, proper representation and documentation should be made in the  transaction documents and the methods should comply with existing laws, rules    and regulations.

We recommended that the City Government carefully evaluate proposed projects for funding out of the tobacco excise tax fund to avoid delay in its implementation and enable the farmers to reap the benefits due them. Management should see to it that the projects are feasible and sustainable and conduct regular monitoring to validate progress of the projects so that the objectives of the fund are achieved.
Also, the City Government should ensure that the proposed projects funded from tobacco excise fund are expressly specified in the AIP pursuant to existing laws, rules and regulations and to avoid duplication of funding proposals and optimize the utilization of government resources. In addition, Management should always secure approval or authorization of the Sanggunian before incurring expenditures and/or entering into contract in the implementation of projects described in generic terms.

We therefore recommended that the City Government maximize the utilization of the Annual City Development Fund to attain desirable socio-economic and environmental outcomes for the city and its intended beneficiaries through identification of specific description, location and target implementation/completion of PPAs under social development, economic development and environmental management sectors that will facilitate implementation and monitoring and that will not necessitate specific approval or authorization from the Sanggunian for every contract.

We therefore recommended that the City Government maximize the utilization of the Annual City Development Fund to attain desirable socio-economic and environmental outcomes for the city and its intended beneficiaries through identification of specific description, location and target implementation/completion of PPAs under social development, economic development and environmental management sectors that will facilitate implementation and monitoring and that will not necessitate specific approval or authorization from the Sanggunian for every contract.

We recommended that the City’s LCPC include only those PPAs consistent with the institutional mechanisms that advocate child rights and interventions similar or closely attributable to those specified in DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-120 dated July 4, 2012.
We therefore recommended that a resolution should be enacted by the Sangguniang Panlungsod for the amount of the honorarium of the appointed OSCA head.

We recommended that the City Budget Officer and City Accountant refrain from treating the honoraria of the OSCA Head as expenditure under Personal Services for proper charging of budgetary accounts and fair presentation of the balances of Honoraria and Other MOOE in the Financial Statements.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct all concerned employees cause the immediate settlement of the disallowance which had been covered by COE and the audit charges which had already become final and executory. It is likewise recommended that Management ensure that suspensions, disallowances and charges are settled in accordance with the provisions of 2009 RRSA prescribed by COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.

observations.reason: Verification of compliance to the audit recommendation disclosed that the values of the accounts affected were already corrected to reflect the mandate of the city ordinance on realty taxes for land properties.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor require the City Treasurer to remit to the National Treasury the unexpended amount of various projects as funded by various NGAs more particularly the unexpended PDAF and other congressional insertions which had been declared as unconstitutional. observations.managementAction: Management returned the unexpended PDAF, Congressional allocations to the National Treasury and Other National Offices for unutilized balances of various projects.
observations.reason: Analysis of accounts revealed that the unexpended PDAF, Congressional allocations and unutilized balances of various projects were remitted to the National Treasury and source agencies.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor make representation with the provincial government to possibly have an agreement that they remit the RPT and SET share and pay the loan amortization as per record of the city. Nonetheless, further validation disclosed that the city government had already coordinated with the PGIS in order to address the issues concerning the recorded payable and receivable accounts of the two LGUs.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the city government stop its present practice of documenting the disbursements for the provision of drugs and medicines to access card holders thru small value procurement to strictly adhere to the above-cited provision of RA 9184. observations.reason: Audit of transactions disclosed that Management had complied with the cited recommendations except the requirement on the presence of Statement of Account for a given period as basis of the City Government in paying for the drugs and medicines dispensed by the drugstore.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management ensure that:

Every Purchase Order be duly filled up with all the data needed as guide for the suppliers in complying with the terms and conditions of the contract. The data is required in order to have sufficient basis to demand from the supplier liquidated damages or penalty of 1/10 of 1% of the cost of undelivered items per day of delay, in case of the latter’s failure to meet the contract time/period.

Brand name and the specific description of the goods procured in the Purchase Orders are indicated to facilitate the inspection and inventory taking of the audit team.
observations.managementAction: The city government was able to completely fill out all the data required in the PO as well as the indication of brand name and specific description of the goods procured.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management stop the practice of procuring goods through reimbursement basis except for petty expenses and that payment should be  made payable to the supplier or creditor in accordance with rules and regulation to avoid possible misuse of funds. Likewise, the City Accountant and City Treasurer should withhold the required taxes in accordance with the Revenue Memorandum Circular and to remit the same to prevent any penalties that may be imposed by BIR.
observations.managementAction: The city government committed to employ ways to prevent the occurrence of reimbursements except those that may be allowed by law and those that are extremely necessary for the welfare of its constituents and in the exigency of service.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management ensure that every construction project to be implemented be provided with CSHP duly approved by the DOLE and to require and monitor the contractors to strictly comply with the requirements in the approved CSHP. Further, we recommend management to deduct the cost of CSHP from the contractors’ billing statement or 10% retention money for non-compliance thereof, otherwise, this will be disallowed in audit.
observations.managementAction: Management was able to comply with the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the city hire a legal officer and to do away with the hiring of private lawyers or law firm to provide for the legal services of the city. observations.managementAction: 2015 AAR Nonexistence of the old PPEs as compared to the inventory of financial reports Due to bulk of transactions of accounts.
observations.reason: The GSO has started with the inventory of PPEs and the City Accountant is in the process of reconciling accounts. However, as of this writing no report and proof of ownership of land has been submitted to this office.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended the City Accountant to reconcile the balances of the accounts with that of the NGOs/POs to reflect the correct balances of the subsidiary as well as the general ledger accounts for fair presentation of the financial statements. Likewise, we have recommended her to provide us the list of recipients for proper confirmation of the recorded balances.
observations.managementAction: 2015 AAR Request for write-off of receivables especially for those that can no longer be reconciled.
observations.reason: Some of the accounts have been reconciled while others can no longer be reconciled due to absence of documents.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended the city accountant to use the asset method in recording the transactions for the procurement of supplies and the accrual basis in accordance with PPSAS No. 1.
observations.managementAction: 2015 AAR Asset method of accounting will be used
observations.reason: The accountant has recorded the procurement using the asset method as per analysis of accounts made.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended the BAC to see to it that all purchases not falling within the exception for bidding under Rule XVI of the IRR be procured through public bidding in order to properly address the government’s thrust on competitiveness on the procurement of goods.
observations.managementAction: 2015 AAR Strict adherence to the APP and prepare plan for possible items for purchase not in the APP.
observations.reason: There are still some purchases which were not made through Public Bidding, the team issued AOM No. 2016-017 for this.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended the CGSO to prepare on a weekly basis the SSMI showing the weekly consumption of supplies for all inventory items and furnish copies thereto to the accountant and this office. The City Accountant should base any adjustment to the inventory accounts on the reported SSMI. Further, the CGSO should prepare the supporting documents in support of the adjustment made by the accountant.
observations.managementAction: 2015 AAR Quarterly release of supplies to each department Adjust inventory report as per CGSO report.
observations.reason: As per verification of ledger accounts, no report and copies of the report was submitted to this office.

observations.recommendation: We have required the city government officials concerned to see to it that all payments due to suppliers and creditors are made through checks in their name in accordance with the provisions of the COA Circular to avoid irregularity in the disbursement of funds. Likewise, the City Accountant and the City Treasurer should withhold the required taxes in accordance with the Revenue Memorandum Circular and to remit the same to prevent any penalties that may be imposed by the Bureau of Internal Revenue.
observations.managementAction: 2015 AAR Contention of the person reimbursing is that when the purchase was paid, tax was already imposed by the supplier.
observations.reason: Though the department and the treasury office want to adhere to the law and regulations, the suppliers do not want to be reimbursing the person the expense does not wanting to have taxes deducted therefrom

observations.recommendation: We have recommended the city government to submit a copy of the APP-CSE for succeeding years to the PS-DBM and procure the same therein to facilitate the procurement process and do away with the rigors of public bidding and other modes of procurement from private entities.
observations.managementAction: 2015 AAR Plan to procure from PS-DBM is done
observations.reason: There has been procurement from the PS-DBM as per information from the city administrator, they have inquired DBM on their procurement for laptop but the specifications requested was not available.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended to the City School Board to limit the utilization of the SEF to the programs and projects defined under RA 7160 and DECS, DBM and DILG Joint Circular No. 01 dated April 14, 1998 in order to maximize the benefits that the students and teachers could avail under this fund.
observations.managementAction: 2015 AAR Local School Board devise annual budget on SEF utilization.
observations.reason: The city have not incurred those expenses charged to SEF as enumerated in our findings.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended the city government and all officials concerned to provide guidelines in the granting of livelihood assistance and to prepare MOA between the parties in order to maximize the use of the fund intended to alleviate the lives of the beneficiaries and to monitor on a regular basis its implementation to be assured of its sustainability.
observations.managementAction: 2015 AAR Formulated guidelines and prepared MOA
observations.reason: For 2016 transactions, no MOA was signed by the parties and no copy of the guidelines have been submitted. See related finding on Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended the CDRRMO to utilize the said fund strictly for calamity-related activities as per LDRRMP so as to accomplish all planned projects and activities and in accordance with R.A. 10121 and its implementing rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: 2015 AAR The unutilized fund shall be taken on the DRRM Plan for the next year as part of the continuing fund.
observations.reason: The balance of LDRRMF as of November 2016 is P 27,271,556.50 out of the total appropriations for C.Y. 2016 in the amount of P 33,089,683.00

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that all due to the provincial government as well as barangays if any be released in accordance with Section 286 (a) of RA 7160. In this manner, the local government unit beneficiary could get hold of the fund and can appropriate such for their operation and other projects that will redound to the constituents. Likewise, reconcile the balances as appearing in the books with that of the balances of the provincial government in order to establish the exact amount to be paid to the local government unit and make necessary adjustment in the books of accounts.
observations.managementAction: 2014 AAR Both parties have not reconciled completely the account due to absence of records.
observations.reason: Both parties have not reconciled the account due to absence of records.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended the head of the procuring entity to ensure that prior to the signing of contracts a performance security is posted in the form and amount prescribed in the IRR in order to protect the interest of the government for possible loss of funds.
observations.managementAction:  Post-audit of transactions revealed that the bidders have complied with the submission of Performance Bond.
observations.reason: Post-audit of transactions revealed that the bidders have complied with the submission of Performance Bond.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended the procuring entity, through the BAC Secretariat to strictly comply with the provision of GPPB Resolution No. 09-2009 as well as Sec. 54.1 of the IRR of R.A. 9184 to attain transparency and competitiveness in the procurement process and in the implementation of procurement contracts and to avoid penalties that maybe imposed thereof.
observations.managementAction: 2014 AAR Most of the purchases amounting to P50,000.00 above has already been posted in the PhilGEPS however some were not posted especially those procured from Non-VAT suppliers and those made through reimbursement.
observations.reason: There has been compliance with the recommendation. There were some contracts not submitted on time but were justified by the officials concerned.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that all copies of approved government contracts, purchase orders and their supporting documents will be submitted to the auditor within five working days upon approval for review to avoid doubts on the validity of the contracts, to ensure the submission of all the necessary supporting documents. Likewise, all documents submitted to them by the bidders should be carefully evaluated and copy of the contracts be properly reviewed to avoid conflicts with the existing provisions of implementing rules on procurement.
observations.managementAction: 2014 AAR There has been substantial compliance with the recommendation. There were some contracts not submitted on time but were justified by the officials concerned.
observations.reason: There has been compliance with the recommendation. There were some contracts not submitted on time but were justified by the officials concerned.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended the agency that prior to the implementation of projects particularly those for the benefit of the barangay should see to it that the facilities and structures be constructed on a publicly-owned land to avoid loss of government funds.
observations.managementAction: 2014 AAR No transaction of that kind has been incurred during the year. A letter has been sent to GSIS for that purpose.
observations.reason: No transaction of that kind has been incurred during the year. A letter has been sent to GSIS for that purpose.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended the concerned officials to refund the amount of overpayment and to comply with the provisions of Local Budget Circular 103 on the grant of RATA to prevent overpayment of RATA particularly the members of the Sanggunian and so with all other officials receiving such benefit. Likewise, for the HRMO to see to it that all leaves of absence of all officials and employees are recorded in their leave cards.
observations.managementAction: 2014 AAR Post-audit of Payroll for RATA reveals that leaves of absences has been deducted from their claim and all leaves of absences have been recorded as such in the leave cards. However, there are some members of the sanggunang panlungsod who were given the whole amount of RATA despite being absent in one or more of the regular sessions held.
observations.reason: Post-audit of Payroll for RATA reveals that leaves of absences has been deducted from their claim and all leaves of absences have been recorded as such in the leave cards. However, there are some members of the sanggunang panlungsod who were given the whole amount of RATA despite being absent in one or more of the regular sessions held.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the City Human Resources and Management Officer see to it that hiring or promotion of employees be meticulously done. Only qualified contenders should be hired and / or promoted to avoid further loss of government funds and to give other more qualified applicants having adequate eligibilities for positions in the government.
observations.managementAction: 2013 AAR The agency has re-appointed these employees to various positions commensurate to the qualifications.
observations.reason: Appointments made during the year has already been attested by the Civil Service Commission.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that management direct the conduct of thorough evaluation / matching of personnel versus work load in order to determine if there are duplication / conflict of functions. The hiring of consultants should be limited to services which can not be provided by the present workforce and only on services which are highly technical or those that can be done only by highly skilled workers.
observations.managementAction: 2013 AAR The city has in fact procured the services of a losing candidate in May 2016 election as consultant.
observations.reason: The city has in fact procured the services of a losing candidate in May 2016 election as consultant.
